files_to_modify.change: Additive only: add frozen dataclasses TokenUsage and CompletionResult, plus a concrete (non-abstract) LLMClient.complete_with_usage() default method that delegates to complete() and returns usage=None. Abstract complete() contract unchanged.

Refactor complete()'s body into a private _do_complete() returning CompletionResult (text, model, usage parsed from response['usage']); complete() becomes a one-line call returning _do_complete(...).text; override complete_with_usage() to return the full CompletionResult. Public complete() signature/behavior/exceptions unchanged.

Same refactor pattern as openrouter_client.py: private _do_complete() parses response['usageMetadata'] in addition to the existing candidates[0].content.parts[0].text path; complete() unchanged in signature/behavior/exceptions.

files_not_modified: agents/llm/ollama_client.py (no change needed: Ollama cost is always 0.0 by convention; complete_with_usage() default from LLMClient base class suffices)
agents/llm/factory.py (no change needed: interceptor takes an already-constructed LLMClient plus an explicit provider label, does not need factory changes)
agents/eval/cost_latency.py (must NOT be modified per acceptance criteria: StageRecord/aggregate_by_stage/rollup_cost_per_1000_queries consumed as-is)
downstream_consumers_checked: agents/llm/test_ollama_client.py, test_openrouter_client.py, test_gemini_client.py, test_provider_selection.py (assert existing complete() request/response shape; must stay green)
new_dependency_risk: none -- stdlib time.perf_counter() + dataclasses + dict rate table only; agents/pyproject.toml untouched
live_api_call_risk: none -- all new/modified tests use httpx.MockTransport, matching existing agents/llm/ test convention; no network access introduced
risk_notes: Refactoring complete() into _do_complete() touches working, already-verified provider clients (4.1.1/4.1.2); mitigated by keeping complete()'s public signature, return type, and exception surface byte-for-byte identical, and re-running the full existing test files unmodified.

Rate table is per-provider+per-model; a paid-provider call for a model not in the table (default or caller-overridden) must fail loudly (raise), not silently emit cost_usd=0 or None, to avoid masking missing pricing data -- mirrors cost_latency.resolve_cost_usd's own philosophy.
